description_long: This object is a single, broad leaf with a heart-shaped outline and a pointed tip, displaying a gradient of green to yellow hues with some brownish patches and small dark spots. The central vein is prominent, running from the base to the tip, with smaller veins branching out symmetrically. The leaf surface appears slightly matte and semi-soft, with a natural, organic texture and some visible imperfections. The edges are smooth and gently curved, and the leaf is relatively flat with a slight natural curl along its length. There is no visible pot, soil, or stem structure, suggesting this is a detached leaf rather than a full potted plant. The coloration and form are typical of a mature, possibly senescing leaf from a broadleaf evergreen or tropical plant.
description: A single, heart-shaped broadleaf with green-yellow coloration, visible veins, and a slightly curled, matte surface. No pot or stem is visible, only the leaf itself.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full heart-shaped leaf, central vein, and color gradient from green to yellow with brown patches.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thinness and slight curl of the leaf, with minimal surface detail visible.
description_view_2: Back view of the leaf, showing a more uniform yellow color and the branching vein structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the leaf's thin profile and gentle curvature.
